author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

`detect_storage_mode` matched `Ok(Some(_))` from `resolve_ref` as
v3 mode without checking the object type. The docstring at
`crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:1037` ("iff the sentinel ref
resolves to a commit") was unenforced; a blob/tree/tag oid
planted via `git update-ref refs/jjf/meta/format-version <oid>`
silently flipped reads to the v3 path.

# Recommendation

Took Option A. Added a `resolve_ref_with_type` helper in
`git.rs` that pairs the existing `rev-parse --verify --quiet`
probe with a `git cat-file -t <oid>` follow-up, and threaded a
new `Error::CorruptSentinel { oid, kind }` through the storage
layer. The CLI envelope kind is `corrupt_sentinel` (exit 1,
details `oid` + `object_type`). New row added to
`docs/cli-json.md`'s error-kind table.

Files changed: `crates/jjf-storage/src/{git.rs,lib.rs}`,
`crates/jjf-storage/tests/v3_read_path.rs`,
`crates/jjf/src/main.rs`, `crates/jjf/tests/ls.rs`,
`docs/cli-json.md`. Two storage-level unit tests (blob and tree
sentinel) plus one CLI integration test (`--json` envelope shape
on `jjf ls`). Landed as commit `f22417da`.

# Confidence

High. Workspace tests: 512 pass. QA harness `c1` no longer
prints `[c1] FINDING: '<verb>' exit 0 ...`; all three verbs
exit 1 with the typed envelope.

# Open follow-ups

None for this bug. The fix is narrow; the helper could be
reused if another caller later needs a "must be a commit"
probe, but no current site needs it.
